sections.label: THINKING
sections.content:
Key observations:
1. Player at rows 32-35, cols 29-31 (N: [[32, 29], [32, 31], [35, 31], [35, 29]])
2. Center frame still at rows 32-35, cols 32-35 (c: [[32, 32], [32, 35], [35, 35], [35, 32]])
3. A NEW white frame appeared at [[32, 24], [32, 28], [35, 28], [35, 24]] - this is where the left frame's white frame moved to!
4. A new blue target appeared at [[33, 25], [33, 26], [34, 26], [34, 25]] - inside the new white frame
5. The left white frame moved from [[28, 16], [28, 20], [31, 20], [31, 16]] to [[32, 24], [32, 28], [35, 28], [35, 24]]
Wait, that's strange. The white frame from the left frame moved? Let me reconsider.
Actually, the white frame at [[32, 24], [32, 28], [35, 28], [35, 24]] is at rows 32-35, cols 24-28. The player is at rows 32-35, cols 29-31. So the white frame is to the left of the player.
But the left frame was at rows 28-31, cols 16-19. The white frame was at [[28, 16], [28, 20], [31, 20], [31, 16]]. Now it's at [[32, 24], [32, 28], [35, 28], [35, 24]]. That's a different position entirely!
Hmm, maybe the white frames move around. Or maybe there's a different mechanic at play.
Actually wait - the center frame is still there (c: [[32, 32], [32, 35], [35, 35], [35, 32]]). The player is at cols 29-31, which is to the left of the center frame at cols 32-35. The player needs to move RIGHT to enter the center frame's space.
Let me move RIGHT once to get to cols 33-35, which would be inside the center frame.

sections.label: TOOL CALL: python
sections.content:
<tool_call>
<function=python>
<parameter=code>
# Player at rows 32-35, cols 29-31
# Center frame at rows 32-35, cols 32-35
# Player is adjacent to center frame, need RIGHT to enter it

action(['RIGHT'])
</parameter>
<parameter=world_model>
